The **Namibia Premiership**, or the **Debmarine Namibia Premiership** for sponsorship reasons, is the highest level of domestic association [football in Namibia](/wiki/Football_in_Namibia "Football in Namibia"). It operates under the auspices of the [Namibia Football Association](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Association "Namibia Football Association").[[1]](#cite_note-From_Premier_League_to_Premiership-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Namibia_Premiership&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

After ongoing financial and organizational problems with the [Namibia Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Premier_League "Namibia Premier League") (NPL), the [Namibia Football Association](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Association "Namibia Football Association") expelled the league and founded a new top-flight league themselves following the [2018–19 season](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_Namibia_Premier_League "2018–19 Namibia Premier League").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-homeless-3)

Despite a planned debut season in 2021, the league finally played its first season in [2022-23](/wiki/2022%E2%80%9323_Namibia_Premier_Football_League "2022–23 Namibia Premier Football League") after securing major sponsorship funding.[[1]](#cite_note-From_Premier_League_to_Premiership-1) Originally called the Namibia Premier Football League, the league was renamed the Namibia Premiership with the significant sponsorship of [N$](/wiki/N$ "N$")13 million provided by Debmarine.[[4]](#cite_note-Debmarine_Namibia_Premiership-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Premiership_Action_Returns-5)

Following the [2023–24 season](/wiki/2023%E2%80%9324_Namibia_Premiership "2023–24 Namibia Premiership"), the Namibia Football Association addressed the long-time issue of clubs with shared ownership competing in the same league. Beginning with the [2024–25 season](/wiki/2024%E2%80%9325_Namibia_Premiership "2024–25 Namibia Premiership"), the practice would no longer be allowed. The debate had begun a season earlier with [Mighty Gunners](/wiki/Mighty_Gunners_F.C. "Mighty Gunners F.C.") and [Military School Okahandja](/wiki/Military_School_Okahandja_FC "Military School Okahandja FC"), both operated by the [Namibian Defence Force](/wiki/Namibian_Defence_Force "Namibian Defence Force"), set to compete in the Premiership. The issue was solved with the sale and rebranding of the club to [Okahandja United](/wiki/Okahandja_United_FC "Okahandja United FC"). The issue arose again in summer 2024 when [Khomas NAMPOL](/wiki/Khomas_NAMPOL_FC "Khomas NAMPOL FC") and [Namibia Correctional Service](/wiki/Namibia_Correctional_Service_FC "Namibia Correctional Service FC") were set to compete in the top-flight. Because of the new policy, Namibian Correctional Service FC were not promoted and were replaced by [First Division South](/wiki/Namibia_First_Division "Namibia First Division") runners-up [Blue Boys](/wiki/Blue_Boys_FC "Blue Boys FC").[[6]](#cite_note-sister_clubs-6)
